title: Lisas Wedding

jokes.text: Zounds! I did thee mightily smitely.
jokes.setup: Ned attempts to speak in Renaissance English
jokes.punchline: Creates the non-word 'smitely' while trying to sound scholarly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders character comedy - his earnest attempt at erudition backfires with a made-up word that sounds plausible

jokes.text: "Zounds" is a Renaissance English word, short for "God's wounds"... but "smitely," that's pure Flanders.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains the etymology of 'Zounds'
jokes.punchline: Identifies Flanders' linguistic invention with deadpan precision
jokes.explanation: Classic Lisa - combines genuine knowledge with sharp character observation. The phrase 'that's pure Flanders' is highly quotable

jokes.text: These Renaissance fairs are so boring. / Oh, really? Did you see the loom?
jokes.setup: Bart complains about boredom at the fair
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sincere enthusiasm for a loom highlights their different interests
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character contrast - Lisa's nerdiness vs Bart's impatience

jokes.text: I took loom in high school.
jokes.setup: Lisa's enthusiasm for the loom
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact claim about taking 'loom' as a class
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan absurdity - treating 'loom' as a regular high school subject with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Yon meat 'tis sweet as summer's wafting breeze... Mine ears are open to the pleas of those who speak ye old English.
jokes.setup: Homer attempts elaborate Renaissance speech to get food
jokes.punchline: His flowery language is motivated purely by hunger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - elaborate effort motivated by simple desire for food

jokes.text: Whatever.
jokes.setup: Homer's elaborate Renaissance speech requesting food
jokes.punchline: Vendor's completely modern, dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation - one word completely undermines Homer's elaborate performance

jokes.text: I've eaten eight different meats. I'm a true Renaissance man.
jokes.setup: Homer's gluttony at the Renaissance fair
jokes.punchline: Misinterprets 'Renaissance man' to mean eating lots of meat
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism - reduces intellectual concept to food consumption

jokes.text: Behold the rarest of the rare... the mythological two-headed hound, born with only one head.
jokes.setup: Promises to show mythical two-headed creature
jokes.punchline: The 'mythological' aspect is that it was born with one head instead of two
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical inversion - the 'mythological' quality is its normalcy, not its strangeness

jokes.text: A horse with the head of a rabbit and the body of a rabbit.
jokes.setup: Introducing the legendary 'esquilax'
jokes.punchline: It's just a rabbit described in the most convoluted way possible
jokes.explanation: One of the show's most quotable lines - perfectly absurd pseudo-mythological description of an ordinary rabbit

jokes.text: It's galloping away. Here, bunny-bunny. Here, bunny. Here, "esquilax."
jokes.setup: The 'esquilax' (rabbit) runs away
jokes.punchline: Barker tries different names to call it back, ending with the made-up term
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the esquilax joke with the barker's desperation

jokes.text: I've been waiting for you, Lisa. / How did you know my name? / Your nametag.
jokes.setup: Fortune teller claims mystical knowledge of Lisa's name
jokes.punchline: Reveals the mundane explanation - her nametag
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection - builds up mystical atmosphere then deflates it with obvious explanation

jokes.text: Wow, you can see into the present.
jokes.setup: Fortune teller accurately describes where Lisa's family members currently are
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sarcastic observation about this 'power'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa skepticism - deflates fortune teller pretensions with precise sarcasm

jokes.text: The happy squirrel. / That's bad? / Possibly. The cards are vague and mysterious.
jokes.setup: Fortune teller draws an innocuous 'happy squirrel' card
jokes.punchline: Even cute imagery is potentially ominous; cards are conveniently ambiguous
jokes.explanation: Good satire of fortune telling's meaningless ambiguity - even happy symbols are threatening

jokes.text: Hey, watch it. / Watch it. / That man is instantly the most annoying person I've ever met.
jokes.setup: Lisa and Hugh bump into each other and both say 'watch it'
jokes.punchline: Lisa's immediate, extreme negative judgment
jokes.explanation: Sets up the romantic comedy trope of initial hatred turning to love, with Lisa's characteristic intensity

jokes.text: A soy-based snack will calm me down.
jokes.setup: Lisa is annoyed by her encounter with Hugh
jokes.punchline: Her solution is very on-brand vegetarian comfort food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - even her stress response is intellectualized and health-conscious

jokes.text: I read at a 78th-grade level.
jokes.setup: Lisa and Hugh are competing over reading speed
jokes.punchline: Lisa's absurdly specific and impossible grade level claim
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Lisa hypercompetitiveness - the specificity of '78th grade' makes it funnier than just 'graduate level'

jokes.text: First, they hate each other. All of a sudden, they love each other. Oh, it doesn't make any sense to me. / Of course not. You're a robot.
jokes.setup: Bart comments on the clichéd romantic progression
jokes.punchline: Lisa dismisses his confusion by calling him a robot
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on romantic comedy tropes, with Lisa's typical intellectual condescension toward Bart

jokes.text: I never met anyone who so understood the magic of Jim Carrey. He can make you laugh with no more than a frantic flailing of his limbs.
jokes.setup: Hugh and Lisa bonding over shared interests
jokes.punchline: Hugh's pretentious analysis of Jim Carrey's physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Hugh's overly intellectual appreciation of lowbrow humor

jokes.text: Yes. Not for their music but for their tireless efforts to preserve historic buildings.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions they both love the Rolling Stones
jokes.punchline: Hugh likes them for architecture preservation, not music
jokes.explanation: Perfect character joke - Hugh's stuffiness extends to appreciating rock stars for their historical preservation work

jokes.text: Yes. It's a good thing they re-evaluated those wacky old designs.
jokes.setup: Looking at futuristic planes in 2010
jokes.punchline: Hugh's understated British reaction to dramatic aircraft redesign
jokes.explanation: Understated British humor about futuristic technology being described as mere design revision

jokes.text: Should I laugh? Was that dry British wit or subtle self-pity? They're staring at me. Better respond.
jokes.setup: Lisa unsure how to interpret Hugh's mother's comment about their dinnerware
jokes.punchline: Lisa's internal monologue reveals her social anxiety
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Lisa's overthinking of social interactions with British upper class

jokes.text: Actually, Lisa, it's just Uncle Eldred. / I get me brain medicines from the National Health.
jokes.setup: Lisa tries to date the painting, thinking it's historical
jokes.punchline: It's just a living relative who speaks from the 'painting'
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - subverts expectation of historical painting with living eccentric relative

jokes.text: Remember, an elevator is a 'lift,' a mile is called a 'kilometer,' and botulism is 'steak-and-kidney pie.'
jokes.setup: Marge giving Lisa British cultural tips
jokes.punchline: Escalates from real British terms to insulting British cuisine
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from helpful translation to food insult, plus Marge gets the kilometer/mile conversion backward

jokes.text: But he left for work a few minutes ago.
jokes.setup: Marge says 'If only your father was still with us'
jokes.punchline: Reveals she just means he went to work, not that he died
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - builds dramatic expectation then deflates it with mundane reality

jokes.text: Oh, no. I just got a touch of the rheumatiz.
jokes.setup: Lisa points out it's a Picturephone
jokes.punchline: Marge pretends her embarrassing appearance is due to 'rheumatiz'
jokes.explanation: Marge's embarrassment about her appearance on video call, with folksy medical excuse

jokes.text: They're finally paying me for this.
jokes.setup: Homer at work in the future
jokes.punchline: Implication he's been working for free all these years
jokes.explanation: Explains Homer's continued employment despite incompetence

jokes.text: Lisa? My one true love. It's not you, Milhouse. I just don't plan to ever get married.
jokes.setup: Milhouse overhears Lisa is getting married
jokes.punchline: Lisa's past rejection of Milhouse's romantic hopes
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Lisa's earlier rejection of marriage, now ironic given her engagement

jokes.text: Lisa? My one true love. It's not you, Milhouse. I just don't plan to ever get married.
jokes.setup: Adult Milhouse still pining for Lisa
jokes.punchline: Lisa's past rejection being proven wrong by current engagement
jokes.explanation: Good character continuity showing Milhouse's persistent devotion and Lisa's ironic situation

jokes.text: Why not have the whole wedding there. We'll do it on a Monday morning. There'll be fewer drunks.
jokes.setup: Barney suggests having the reception at Moe's
jokes.punchline: His logic about fewer drunks on Monday morning at a bar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney logic - drunkard's practical advice about avoiding other drunks

jokes.text: 'Whereas Homer Simpson...' Well, these seem to be in order. I'll be in the hammock.
jokes.setup: Lisa presents court order preventing Homer from planning wedding
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately accepts it without reading and goes to relax
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - accepts legal document without reading it, immediately prioritizes relaxation

jokes.text: Oh, Mr. Burns, we'll thaw you out the second they discover the cure for 17 stab wounds in the back. How we doing, boys? / Well, we're up to 15.
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns is frozen, waiting for medical advances
jokes.punchline: They're making progress on treating multiple stab wounds
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Burns' inevitable demise, with absurd medical specificity about stab wounds

jokes.text: If you have three Pepsis and drink one, how much more refreshed are you?
jokes.setup: Future math class with corporate sponsorship
jokes.punchline: Math problems replaced with advertising-based questions
jokes.explanation: Satirical prediction about corporate influence in education

jokes.text: It must be Lisa Simpson. Of course, Martin Prince perished in that science-fair explosion. / Not quite perished, my lady love. Although some days I wish I had.
jokes.setup: Teacher assumes Lisa is their only literate graduate
jokes.punchline: Martin reveals he survived but is miserable
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Martin's survival being worse than death, maintaining his pretentious speech pattern

jokes.text: No. Look at this license. I'm Mohamed Jafar. / Quimby, after this fare, get your indicted ass to the convention center.
jokes.setup: Quimby working as taxi driver under false identity
jokes.punchline: Dispatcher calls him by real name and references his legal troubles
jokes.explanation: Good political corruption joke - even his fake identity cover is blown

jokes.text: As long as they're not squatting in a ditch, poking berries up their noses. / And if they are? / Here they come. Raise the flag.
jokes.setup: Hugh sets low expectations for Lisa's family
jokes.punchline: Lisa asks what if they are, Hugh immediately signals surrender
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Hugh's snobbery and immediate surrender when faced with worst-case scenario

jokes.text: If the building inspector comes by, it's not a room, it's a window box.
jokes.setup: Homer shows Hugh the guest bedroom he built
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's an illegal construction with absurd cover story
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer incompetence - illegal room construction with hilariously inadequate cover story

jokes.text: I also crush cars into cubes. On the side, I promote local tough-man contests. I'm getting out all my aggression till I go to law school.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his various jobs to Hugh
jokes.punchline: Claims violent work is therapy before law school
jokes.explanation: Good Homer delusion - framing destructive work as self-improvement before impossible career change

jokes.text: I'm getting out all my aggression till I go to law school.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his violent career choices
jokes.punchline: He plans to become a lawyer - another aggressive profession
jokes.explanation: Ironic career plan that maintains Homer's aggressive nature in professional form

jokes.text: Heather Locklear Fortensky remains at large.
jokes.setup: News about celebrity arrests
jokes.punchline: Future celebrity name combining multiple 90s references
jokes.explanation: Future prediction humor combining celebrity name trends

jokes.text: You know, we saved your ass in World War II. Well, we saved your ass in World War III.
jokes.setup: American making typical WWII comment to Brit
jokes.punchline: Hugh's polite counter about a future war
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of typical American-British historical argument into future territory

jokes.text: You know, Fox turned into a hardcore-sex channel so gradually, I didn't even notice.
jokes.setup: Casual bar conversation
jokes.punchline: Meta joke about Fox network's programming evolution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on Fox's programming - became one of the most quoted Simpsons lines ever

jokes.text: When we hid in the Dumpster... after the fire alarm went off in the pornographic-magazine warehouse.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking how Hugh got injured
jokes.punchline: Elaborate explanation involving increasingly absurd circumstances
jokes.explanation: Shows the chaos that follows Homer and Bart everywhere

jokes.text: Oh, Milhouse doesn't count.
jokes.setup: Lisa worries about wearing white because of Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Marge's dismissive response to Lisa's concerns about Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Perfect motherly dismissal of Milhouse's romantic significance - brutally efficient

jokes.text: Oh, Milhouse doesn't count.
jokes.setup: Lisa's concern about wearing white after Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Marge's dismissive response about Milhouse's significance
jokes.explanation: Perfect maternal dismissal - brutally efficient and character-perfect

jokes.text: I met a really nice exotic dancer at Hugh's bachelor party. / Hugh didn't have a bachelor party. / We had one in his honor. / I had one in his honor.
jokes.setup: Homer mentions meeting someone at Hugh's bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Reveals he threw himself a bachelor party in Hugh's honor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - creates bachelor party for someone else as excuse for his own behavior

jokes.text: I had one in his honor. I went to a strip club.
jokes.setup: Clarifying about Hugh's bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Homer threw himself a party in Hugh's honor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - any excuse for strip club visit

jokes.text: Octopussy. Man, I must have seen that movie twice.
jokes.setup: Homer complimenting the English to Hugh's parents
jokes.punchline: His underwhelming praise for seeing a movie only twice
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at cultural appreciation - 'twice' delivered as if it's a huge number

jokes.text: I can't feel anything below my cummerbund.
jokes.setup: Burns partially defrosted and seated at wedding
jokes.punchline: His formal way of describing paralysis using fancy clothing terminology
jokes.explanation: Burns uses formal terminology even when describing medical problems - perfectly in character

jokes.text: I always felt you were the best thing my name ever got attached to.
jokes.setup: Homer giving Lisa a heartfelt pre-wedding speech
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about his own life achievements
jokes.explanation: Touching moment undercut by Homer's self-deprecation

jokes.text: Since you learned to pin your own diapers, you've been smarter than me.
jokes.setup: Homer giving Lisa an emotional father-daughter speech
jokes.punchline: Admits she's been smarter since infancy in Homer-specific way
jokes.explanation: Sweet Homer moment with characteristically specific and slightly inappropriate detail

jokes.text: I was just humoring him. Actually, he frightens me a bit.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks why Hugh won't wear Homer's cuff links
jokes.punchline: Hugh admits he was lying and finds Homer scary
jokes.explanation: Hugh's snobbery and cowardice exposed - breaks promise due to social embarrassment

jokes.text: I've attempted to enjoy your family on a personal level, on an ironic level, as a novelty, as camp, as kitsch, as cautionary example. Nothing works.
jokes.setup: Hugh explaining his relationship with Lisa's family
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly pretentious analytical frameworks, all unsuccessful
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating list of increasingly pretentious ways to appreciate the Simpsons - perfect character assassination of Hugh's intellectual snobbery

jokes.text: You're like a flower that grew out of dirt.
jokes.setup: Hugh trying to compliment Lisa
jokes.punchline: His backhanded compliment calling her family 'dirt'
jokes.explanation: Hugh's condescension revealed in what he thinks is a romantic metaphor - insults family while complimenting Lisa

jokes.text: It never would've happened if the wedding was in the church with God instead of out here in the cheap showiness of nature.
jokes.setup: Wedding is called off outdoors
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy blames nature for being too showy compared to church
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lovejoy - condemns nature as 'cheap showiness' while promoting institutional religion

jokes.text: But I specialize in foretelling the relationships where you get jerked around.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks about her true love after the failed engagement
jokes.punchline: Fortune teller's honest admission about her specialty
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the episode's structure while maintaining fortune teller character

jokes.text: I ate seven pounds of fudge. The man at the stand said it was a record.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks what Homer did while she was gone
jokes.punchline: Homer's proud achievement of excessive fudge consumption
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer gluttony presented as achievement - perfect character consistency

tmdb_overview: A fortuneteller predicts Lisa's engagement and wedding to a fellow student from an upper-crust British family.
insight: Future-episode format softens punchlines: 73.6 score despite solid 2.99 jokes-per-minute density.
